acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] CLI module tree moved
- [ ] Command tests compile and pass
- [ ] Imports respect DAG constraints
closed_reason: Acceptance criteria met; CLI module migration complete with boundary + verification gates green
description:
**Problem**
Command implementation locality is blocked by monolithic layout.
design: Relocate CLI parser/command/render modules into `beads-cli` with typed interfaces.

notes.author: darin@darins-Mac-Studio-2.local
notes.content: Progress: moved cli helper modules (filters/parsers/validation) into crates/beads-cli with compatibility wrappers in crates/beads-rs/src/cli/*. This keeps behavior stable while shrinking beads-rs CLI ownership. Verified cargo check -p beads-cli and -p beads-rs plus CLI unit tests.

notes.author: darin@darins-Mac-Studio-2.local
notes.content: Progress: removed beads-rs CLI compatibility wrapper modules for prime/setup/onboard and rewired runtime dispatch directly to beads-cli command types/handlers from cli::run. Deleted crates/beads-rs/src/cli/commands/{prime,setup,onboard}.rs and updated command typing in commands/mod.rs. Verified cargo check -p beads-rs, cargo test -p beads-cli, and cargo check --workspace.

notes.author: darin@darins-Mac-Studio-2.local
notes.content:
Major progress: removed beads-rs CLI shim modules `src/cli/{parsers,filters,validation}.rs` and rewired command handlers directly to `beads_cli::{parsers,filters,validation}` APIs. Also removed prime/setup/onboard shim command modules earlier and dispatches directly through beads-cli-backed handlers/types. No behavior changes observed in CLI critical path tests.
Validation: cargo check -p beads-rs, cargo test -p beads-rs --test integration -- cli::critical_path:: (6 pass), just dylint, cargo check --workspace.

notes.author: darin@darins-Mac-Studio-2.local
notes.content:
Update: mapped the hard cycle blocker for full CLI handler migration and converted .22 into an executable dependency chain under the epic.

Cycle blocker observed:
- current edge: beads-rs -> beads-cli
- full handler move needs upgrade/store/migrate host operations still owned by beads-rs
- adding beads-cli -> beads-rs would create forbidden cycle

To execute without shims/cycles, I created and claimed these phased beads:
- bd-21eg.37 (closed): extract shared CLI runtime primitives into beads-cli
- bd-21eg.38 (in_progress): move pure IPC read-only handlers into beads-cli + rewire dispatch
- bd-21eg.39 (open): add typed backend trait for host-coupled ops (upgrade/store/migrate)
- bd-21eg.40 (open): finalize ownership and delete migrated beads-rs handler modules

Dependency wiring:
- .38 depends on .37
- .39 depends on .37
- .40 depends on .38 + .39
- .22 depends on .40

This keeps .22 focused on final outcome while execution proceeds in typed, non-cyclic increments.

notes.author: darin@darins-Mac-Studio-2.local
notes.content:
Closeout evidence for bd-21eg.22:

- CLI module tree is owned by `crates/beads-cli` (`commands/`, `parsers.rs`, `filters.rs`, `runtime.rs`, `upgrade/`, `migrate/`).
- Final CLI ownership cleanup beads (`bd-21eg.38`/`.39`/`.40`) are complete, with migrated handlers removed from `beads-rs` shim ownership.
- DAG/boundary invariant holds:
  - `rg -n "crate::daemon::" crates/beads-rs/src/cli crates/beads-cli/src` => no matches.
  - `just dylint` passes.
- Verification for command/test stability passes:
  - `cargo check`
  - `cargo fmt --all`
  - `just dylint`
  - `cargo clippy --all-features -- -D warnings`
  - `cargo test`
  - `RUST_TEST_THREADS=1 cargo test --features slow-tests`

Acceptance mapping:
- [x] CLI module tree moved
- [x] Command tests compile and pass
- [x] Imports respect DAG constraints

No intended behavior changes; CLI critical-path and integration coverage stayed green.

title: Move CLI module tree and command handlers into beads-cli

acceptance_criteria:
**Acceptance**
- [ ] A `ReadScope` validated type exists with a single constructor that normalizes inputs.
- [ ] Daemon entrypoints use `ReadScope` and do not re-normalize namespace/consistency.
- [ ] `normalize_read_consistency` / `normalize_namespace` usage is reduced to the constructor or removed.
- [ ] Tests cover invalid namespace/consistency rejection and valid normalization.
description:
**Problem**
Read-scope normalization (namespace + read consistency) is scattered across daemon entrypoints:
- `normalize_read_consistency` and `normalize_namespace` in `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/coord.rs`
- Callers in `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/admin.rs` and `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/server.rs` repeatedly normalize inputs

This is scatter + implicit: every new entrypoint must remember to normalize, and there is no type that represents a validated read scope.

**Files:**
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/coord.rs`
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/admin.rs`
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/server.rs`
- `crates/beads-core/src/namespace.rs` (if adding shared type)
design:
**Design**
Introduce a validated `ReadScope` type and require it at all read entrypoints.

Concrete plan:
1) Define `ReadScope` (namespace + consistency) in `beads-core` or a shared daemon module.
2) Provide a constructor that performs normalization and validation once.
3) Update daemon entrypoints to accept `ReadScope` instead of raw namespace/consistency fields.
4) Remove repeated normalization calls in `admin`, `server`, and `coord` once the type is in place.

**Design Notes**
- The type should expose canonical namespace and consistency values so downstream code can trust them.
- Keep error reporting precise (field names and reasons).
title: Validated ReadScope for daemon entrypoints
